description_long: This is a standard dining fork with a sleek, elongated handle that gently tapers toward the head. The fork features four evenly spaced, straight tines that are slightly curved upward at the tips, ideal for piercing and holding food. The handle is smooth and widens subtly at the end, providing a comfortable grip. The entire object appears to be made of polished stainless steel, giving it a uniform metallic gray color and a reflective surface. There are no visible decorative engravings, branding, or embellishments on the handle or tines. The fork is depicted against a plain white background, with no contextual elements present.
description: A polished stainless steel fork with four straight tines and a smooth, gently tapered handle, finished in metallic gray.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the fork, with all four tines clearly visible and the handle tapering downward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the gentle upward curve of the tines and the slim, elongated handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the tines and the handle's subtle widening at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the curvature of the tines and the handle's sleek, continuous form.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is elongated, gently tapers toward the head, and subtly widens at the base with a smooth, rounded cross-section and no visible decorative features.
attribute_tine_alignment: The tines are straight, parallel, and evenly spaced, with a slight upward curve at the tips and no irregularities.
